subject: LU-4330 lnet: Allocate MEs and small MDs in own kmem_caches

values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Patch Set 7:

With this patch do you still memory corruption in time? Recently a patch landed upstream for ko2iblnd that exposed a nasty memory corruption. I wonder if the corruption is coming from there.

Patch Set 7:

James, in my case the corruption is ext4/ldiskfs related, see LU-7980. So yes, after this patch we still see corruptions, but no longer affecting LNet MEs/small-MDs ;-)

Patch Set 7:

Patch Set 11: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

The patch looks like a reasonable cleanup, and these structures are allocated often enough that they can likely benefit from their own slabs.

The code could be improved by changing the static inline code to be proper functions in a .c file, but that could be a separate patch.

Are you still planning to land this, or should it be abandoned in favour of http://review.whamcloud.com/18754 which fixes the actual source of the corruption?
Patch Set 11: Code-Review+1

Patch Set 11:

Andreas, yes I still want this patch to land, because I originally developed it for an other corruption cause than the one to be fixed by http://review.whamcloud.com/19349/ master patch (http://review.whamcloud.com/18754/ is its b_ieel2_0 back-port). So seems that the <size-128> generic kmem_cache can be of a frequent usage/victim by others Kernel pieces, so at least 2 good reasons to move LNet MEs/small-MDs out of it, this may reduce contention during their [de-]allocations and may be to avoid Lustre code to be pointed as guilty causing related crashes ;-)

parents.subject: LU-7975 lod: fix delayed stripe error path & Client resend

commit.subject: LU-4330 lnet: Allocate MEs and small MDs in own kmem_caches
commit.message:
LU-4330 lnet: Allocate MEs and small MDs in own kmem_caches

As part of LU-3848 and LU-4330, it has been discovered that LNET
MEs and small MDs (<=128 Bytes) are allocated in <size-128> kmem_cache
and thus can suffer quite frequent corruptions, from other modules or
Kernel parts, that occur there. To avoid this, MEs and small-MDs
specific kmem_cache have been created.

Also, lnet_libmd struct fields have been re-ordered to optimize its
memory foot-print.

Signed-off-by: Bruno Faccini <bruno.faccini@intel.com>
Change-Id: I6cfbed0c3f0a4c2447fe22b2d0d30dba8e3f3173
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/18586
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Doug Oucharek <doug.s.oucharek@intel.com>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
